headlines.title: Refugees would be told to repay around £10,000 under new asylum bill
headlines.description: The Home Office will be given powers to recover costs from adults who have received asylum support under new laws.

headlines.title: Manhunt under way in France after bomb injures Ukrainian oligarch and family in Monaco
headlines.description: The blast was caused by an explosive device which appeared to contain bolts and pellets, the head of Monaco's government said.

headlines.title: One big win and three defeats for Trump in dramatic day at Supreme Court
headlines.description: While Trump celebrated a ruling expanding presidential power to remove and replace regulators, other decisions were major setbacks.

headlines.title: Homes harder to sell as high mortgage rates frustrate buyers
headlines.description: Three in five homes listed for sale since January remain on the market, says property portal Zoopla.

headlines.title: 'No rescuers have been sent': Venezuelans accuse government of negligence over earthquake response
headlines.description: People in areas devastated by twin earthquakes say they need more support from the government.

headlines.title: Another UK heatwave could be on the way
headlines.description: As parts of Europe continue to swelter in record temperatures, higher temperatures could develop again across the UK later this week, as Simon King explains.

headlines.title: Pupil put in isolation booth for more than half a school year, BBC learns
headlines.description: The pupil's experience in a Yorkshire school highlights concerns about zero-tolerance behaviour policies.

headlines.title: Man charged with attempted murder and arson following Belfast disorder
headlines.description: The 29-year-old was arrested on Monday in connection with a glider bus which was set on fire on 9 June.

headlines.title: US security chief 'danced happy dance' after Iran exit
headlines.description: The United States' head of homeland security says he "danced a happy dance" when Iran's elimination from the World Cup was confirmed.

headlines.title: The end for Nagelsmann? Germany suffer their 'next football nightmare'
headlines.description: Germany make a shock last-32 exit against Paraguay, losing a World Cup penalty shootout for the first time to pile pressure on boss Julian Nagelsmann.

headlines.title: Pick Kobbie Mainoo over Elliot Anderson against DR Congo, says Wayne Rooney
headlines.description: Former England captain Wayne Rooney says he would start Manchester United midfielder Kobbie Mainoo instead of Elliot Anderson in Wednesday's World Cup last-32 tie with DR Congo.

headlines.title: Why are South Korean fans so angry at their football coach?
headlines.description: Hong Myung-bo has resigned after South Korea failed to advance to the World Cup knockout stages.

headlines.title: Rescuers keep up search for survivors at collapsed La Guaira building
headlines.description: At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.

headlines.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
headlines.description: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.

headlines.title: 'No one move!': The agonising silence as Venezuela rescuers listen for survivors
headlines.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.

headlines.title: Dogs, drones and sound detectors: How rescuers search for quake survivors
headlines.description: Teams armed with a range of techniques continue to dig through the rubble in Venezuela.

headlines.title: We had packed lunches every day for 10 years and retired at 40
headlines.description: The Fire (Financially Independent, Retire Early) movement sees followers save as much as possible.

headlines.title: Chris Mason: Burnham revels on public stage but has little time to hammer ideas into shape
headlines.description: As a man who has held prime ministerial ambitions for years, Andy Burnham makes his dash for the door, political editor Chris Mason writes.

headlines.title: Is tennis' relentless nature causing injury crisis?
headlines.description: After British stars Jack Draper and Emma Raducanu withdrew injured from Wimbledon, BBC Sport analyses why so many top players are struggling with injury.

headlines.title: In pictures: Stunning Strawberry Moon lights up the sky
headlines.description: Clear night skies over Hampshire and Dorset give stunning shots of the Strawberry Moon.

headlines.title: Why £15 durians are being sold at half price or given away for free
headlines.description: As Malaysian durians are being sold at throwaway prices, farmers worry about their livelihoods.

headlines.title: Lily Allen defends tour after some fans criticise show length
headlines.description: Some fans had complained that the show is too short because the singer only performs her latest album.

headlines.title: Shetland set to back £1.5bn plan to connect islands with undersea tunnels
headlines.description: The undersea links between some of the UK's most northerly islands could be in place within eight years.

headlines.title: Heavy security in South Africa as anti-migrant protesters take to the streets
headlines.description: Thousands of people have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.

headlines.title: WhatsApp to let people chat by swapping usernames instead of phone numbers

headlines.title: Boy says it was incredible bumping into princess doing Three Peaks Challenge
headlines.description: Ted and his father say it was incredible bumping into Catherine, Princess of Wales.

headlines.title: Parents urge council not to shut Scotland's smallest secondary

headlines.title: Why a mural of Eastenders legend Dot Cotton has appeared in a Welsh village

headlines.description: The new Nations Championship pits the northern hemisphere against the south. Traditionally there would only be one winner in such a contest. Perhaps things are different now...
